data_contracts_notes: Partially present - mentions PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY but doesn't detail the contract for getContextEnvVar() function signature, return types, or full list of affected env vars
dependency_context_notes: Present but minimal - references getContextEnvVar() function and Vite/Astro Node adapter, but no file paths or detailed integration points
behavioral_specificity_notes: Describes the fallback order (import.meta.env.PUBLIC_* → process.env) but doesn't specify exact precedence rules, timing, or behavior when vars exist in multiple places

PRESENT: Root cause: import.meta.env.PUBLIC_* is statically replaced at build time by Vite, making runtime values inaccessible
Specific env var name affected: PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
Solution: add process.env as third fallback in getContextEnvVar()
Context: Astro Node adapter runtime scenario
Mentions tests were added for environment variable precedence
PARTIALLY_PRESENT: Scope: mentions getContextEnvVar() but doesn't list all affected env vars or functions
Acceptance criteria: PR checklist exists but doesn't define specific test cases or pass conditions
Data contracts: mentions PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY but doesn't detail function signature or return behavior
ABSENT: Error handling: no mention of what happens if process.env vars are malformed or unavailable
Edge cases: no discussion of precedence conflicts (what if var exists in multiple sources?)
Boundary conditions: no limits on which env vars can be read, timing constraints, or security implications
Explicit test cases: PR mentions tests added but doesn't specify Given/When/Then scenarios
Performance implications: no discussion of lookup overhead
Rollout/migration strategy: no guidance for users on how this changes behavior
File paths: no reference to which files contain getContextEnvVar()
